hypothesis: In REINVENT4 singlet fission optimization, replacing fixed NaviDiv thresholds with a closed-loop controller that adaptively tunes per-step penalty weights to maintain the mean pairwise Morgan-fingerprint Tanimoto similarity at 0.12±0.01 will preserve scaffold count and fragment uniqueness at levels not significantly lower than those under the Combined-constraints regime while achieving a final average property score not significantly lower than the Baseline (two-sided t-test, p>0.05 across five random seeds).
